//: Scope config for acceptance/test-repo-standard.mjs — the committed repo-hygiene lint.
//about: The CANON (six Keep a Changelog categories, standard-readme structure with License last, the voice ban) is hardcoded in the lint; this file tunes SCOPE only. Keys starting with // are operator comments; unknown keys are hard errors. Deleting this file still runs the full canon, but with the lint's laxer built-in scope (no counts, no extra sections, minSections 1) — this shipped file IS the stricter house default.
//docs: Paths of the governed meta docs. 'extra' adds more files to the voice/count sweeps.
//manifest: A provenance/inventory doc whose Totals line must reconcile with its own table rows, e.g. { "file": "COPY-MANIFEST.md", "statuses": ["UNCHANGED", "MODIFIED", "NEW"] }. false = this repo has none (the check prints SKIP, never a silent pass).
//versionManifest: Where the released version lives, for the CHANGELOG lockstep. Omit to auto-detect (.claude-plugin/plugin.json, then package.json); a path string for a JSON manifest with .version; { "file": ..., "match": "regex with one capture" } for non-JSON; false if the repo has no version manifest.
//readme: Extra required README sections beyond the hardcoded canon, as case-insensitive regexes over H2 headings. The scaffolder ships the full standard-readme + honesty shape.
//conventions: The `## N.` contiguity rule is hardcoded; minSections is an anti-gutting floor.
//voice: extraBanned appends to the hardcoded marketing-ban list (each extra term must also be documented in CONVENTIONS — the reflexivity check enforces that). alsoScan widens the sweep.
//counts: Machine-checked numeric claims, keyed by a stable id. Each: pattern (regex, group 1 captures the number), and a source of truth — glob (literal dirs + '*' in the basename only) or file+lineRegex — or neither for cross-doc-agreement-only numbers. Optional docs (default: the whole meta-doc set) and minMentions. Declare a count ONLY when the repo actually states it in prose.
//stableDocs: Rarely-changing meta files that must exist and open with an H1. Replacing this list is a visible weakening — think before shrinking it.
//checks: The only off switch, per check id, and it is loud: { "<id>": { "enabled": false, "why": "<reason printed on every run>" } }. Ids: changelog, lockstep, readme, conventions, manifest, voice, counts, reflexivity, stable-docs, todos.
